right.e: The answer on the test was right.

really.e: The new movie was really funny.

real.e: The real dinosaur bones are kept in the museum.

read.d: To look at words and understand them
read.e: I like to read books about animals.

run.d: To move quickly with your legs
run.e: The kids like to run and play outside.

room.d: a space inside a building with walls and a ceiling
room.e: My little sister's room has lots of toys in it.

remember.d: to keep something in your mind
remember.e: I will remember to do my homework before playing.

research.e: I do research to learn new things.

rather.e: I would rather play outside than watch TV.

reason.d: The cause or explanation for something
reason.e: There was a good reason why I was late to school.

red.d: A bright color, like an apple or a fire truck
red.e: The little boy's favorite color is red.

report.d: Information that is given about something
report.e: The teacher asked the students to write a report about their favorite animal.

ready.d: Prepared and able to do something
ready.e: I'm ready to go to the park and play.

return.d: To go back to a place or person
return.e: I need to return the book to the library.

role.d: The job or part that someone has in a group or activity
role.e: As the team captain, my role is to lead and help my teammates.

rest.d: To relax and recover your energy
rest.e: After a long day, I like to rest on the couch.

result.d: What happens or comes out of an action or event
result.e: The result of my science experiment was very interesting.

results.d: What happens after you do something
results.e: The team was happy with the good results of the game.

road.d: The path cars and people use to get places
road.e: They drove down the long road to get to the beach.

rights.d: Things you are allowed to do
rights.e: Everyone has the right to go to school.

received.d: To get or be given something
received.e: I received a new book from my teacher.

rate.d: The speed something happens or moves at.
rate.e: The car was going at a fast rate down the road.

record.d: To write down or store information so you can look at it later.
record.e: The teacher asked the students to record their answers in their notebooks.

running.d: When you move your legs quickly to go from one place to another.
running.e: The little boy was running to catch the bus.

re.e: I need to re-read the book to understand it better.

relationship.d: The way two people know and care about each other
relationship.e: My mom and dad have a loving relationship.

round.e: The ball was round and bouncy.

range.d: A group of things that are of the same type
range.e: The restaurant has a wide range of menu items to choose from.

region.d: A large area of land that has its own special features
region.e: The mountains in that region of the country are very tall.

risk.d: The chance that something bad might happen.
risk.e: Going down the steep hill is a big risk.

related.d: When things are connected or have a link
related.e: My sister and I are related because we are family.

review.d: To look at something again carefully
review.e: I will review my spelling words before the test.

rules.d: Instructions on how to do something
rules.e: We have to follow the rules in our classroom.

reading.d: The act of looking at and understanding words.

release.d: To let something go or make it available.
release.e: The movie studio will release the new film next month.

radio.d: A device that plays music and news through speakers
radio.e: I listen to the radio while I'm driving in the car.

respect.d: Treating someone or something in a kind and polite way
respect.e: I always show respect to my teachers.

race.d: A competition to see who is the fastest
race.e: The runners raced to see who could cross the finish line first.

reported.d: When someone tells others about something that happened
reported.e: The news reporter reported on the big storm that hit the town.

response.d: An answer or reaction to something
response.e: The teacher gave a quick response to the student's question.

recent.d: Happening or appearing not long ago
recent.e: The new toy is a recent gift from my grandparents.

required.d: Needed or necessary to do something
required.e: I am required to do my homework before playing.

river.d: A large natural stream of water flowing in a channel to the sea, a lake, or another river
river.e: The mighty Mississippi River flows through many states.

rock.d: a hard, heavy thing found in the earth
rock.e: We found a big rock to sit on while we had a picnic.

released.d: When something is allowed to go free
released.e: The zookeeper released the animal back into the wild.

rule.d: A guideline that tells you what to do
rule.e: The classroom rule is to raise your hand before speaking.

recently.d: Not long ago, a short time before now
recently.e: I just visited the museum recently.

russian.e: The family enjoys eating Russian food.

reasons.d: The explanations or causes for something
reasons.e: I have several good reasons for wanting a pet dog.

reports.d: Information that tells you what happened
reports.e: The news report gave details about the big storm.

reach.d: to be able to get or touch something
reach.e: The kid couldn't reach the top shelf to get the book.

religious.d: believing in and practicing a religion
religious.e: The family went to the religious ceremony at the church.

regular.d: Something that happens often or is normal
regular.e: I have a regular bedtime of 8 o'clock every night.

reached.d: to stretch out and grab something
reached.e: The tall boy reached the cookie jar on the top shelf.

reality.d: the way things really are, not just imagined
reality.e: I knew the monster under my bed wasn't real in reality.

remain.d: to stay in the same place or condition
remain.e: The last piece of cake remained on the plate.

rich.d: having a lot of money or valuable things
rich.e: The king was so rich that he had a large castle.

remains.d: What is left of something after the rest is gone.
remains.e: After the party, the food remains on the table.

royal.d: Having to do with a king, queen, or other important leader.
royal.e: The royal family lives in a big castle.

ran.d: When someone or something moves quickly by putting one foot in front of the other.
ran.e: The dog ran across the yard to greet me.

resources.d: Supplies or materials that are needed for a purpose.
resources.e: The teacher used art resources to decorate the classroom.

rates.d: The cost or speed of something
rates.e: The rent rates are high in the city.

records.d: Information that is written down and kept
records.e: The school keeps records of all the students.

responsible.d: Able to be trusted to do the right thing
responsible.e: The student was responsible for cleaning the classroom.

russia.d: A very large country in Europe and Asia
russia.e: My family visited Russia last summer and saw the famous Red Square.

raised.d: When something goes up to a higher place
raised.e: My hand was raised to answer the teacher's question.

returned.d: When you come back to a place after being away
returned.e: I returned home from school feeling happy.

rise.d: To move or stand up from a lower position.
rise.e: The sun will rise in the morning.

reference.d: Information that helps you learn about something
reference.e: I use a dictionary as a reference when I need to spell a word.

rose.e: The rose in the garden has bright red petals.

realize.d: To understand or become aware of something
realize.e: The girl realized she left her backpack at school.

receive.d: To get or accept something that is given to you
receive.e: The boy received a new toy for his birthday.

regional.d: Relating to or typical of a particular area or region
regional.e: The restaurant serves regional dishes from the southern part of the country.

religion.d: A belief system and way of life that people follow
religion.e: Many people in the town practice the same religion.

robert.e: Robert is my best friend from school.

removed.d: To take something away or to a different place
removed.e: The teacher removed the book from the student's desk.

ride.d: To travel in or on something
ride.e: The children liked to ride their bikes.

ring.d: A round shape that you can wear on your finger
ring.e: I have a shiny gold ring on my finger.

runs.d: When someone or something moves quickly
runs.e: The dog runs fast in the park.

republic.d: A country where the people choose their leaders through voting
republic.e: The United States is a democratic republic.

relations.d: The way people are connected or how things are connected
relations.e: My family has good relations with our neighbors.

richard.e: My friend Richard likes to play soccer with me.

require.d: To need or demand something in order to happen.
require.e: The recipe requires milk, eggs, and flour to make the cake.

responsibility.d: A duty or job that you must do carefully.
responsibility.e: Taking care of the class pet is my responsibility.

raise.d: To lift or move something higher up.
raise.e: I need to raise my hand to answer the question.

rare.d: not common, something that doesn't happen often
rare.e: A rainbow is a rare sight in the desert.

remained.d: to continue to be in the same place
remained.e: The book remained on the shelf all day.

reduce.d: to make something smaller or less in amount
reduce.e: Eating more vegetables can help reduce the amount of sugar in your diet.

rain.d: water that falls from the sky
rain.e: I love to listen to the rain pattering on the window.

reaction.d: a response to something that happens
reaction.e: My reaction to the good news was to jump up and down.

reduced.e: The school lunch menu has reduced the amount of sugar.

roll.d: to move by turning over and over
roll.e: The ball started to roll down the hill.

route.d: A way to get from one place to another
route.e: We took the scenic route to the beach.

relationships.d: The way two or more people or things are connected.
relationships.e: My sister has a good relationship with our neighbor.

relatively.d: Compared to something else, but not exactly the same.
relatively.e: The new park is relatively close to my house.

request.e: I will request a new book from the library.

revolution.d: A big change or shift in the way things are done.
revolution.e: The computer revolution changed how we use technology.

recorded.d: To capture sound or video and save it.
recorded.e: The TV show was recorded so I could watch it later.

referred.d: To send someone to another person or place for help.
referred.e: The doctor referred me to a specialist.

remove.d: To take something away or off of something else.
remove.e: I need to remove the stain from my shirt.

requires.d: To need or demand something in order to happen.
requires.e: Baking a cake requires following the recipe.

restaurant.d: A place where you can go to eat food that is cooked and served to you.
restaurant.e: We went to a nice restaurant for dinner.

remaining.d: Remaining means the parts or things that are left over.
remaining.e: I have three cookies remaining from my snack.

ray.d: a line of light coming from the sun or another bright object
ray.e: The sunlight made a bright ray on the floor.

regarding.e: I am writing to you regarding our meeting.

reporting.e: The news reporter is reporting on the weather.

rural.d: relating to the countryside, not the city
rural.e: Many farms are located in the rural area.

reform.d: to change something to make it better
reform.e: The school plans to reform the lunch menu.

revealed.d: to show or tell something that was hidden
revealed.e: The magician revealed the secret behind the trick.

represent.d: To speak or act on behalf of someone or something
represent.e: The student council members represent the ideas of all the students.

relevant.d: important or useful for a particular situation
relevant.e: The information in this book is very relevant to my school project.

represented.d: to be a sign or symbol of something
represented.e: The flag represents the country of Canada.

reserve.d: to save or set aside something for a special use
reserve.e: I need to reserve a table at the restaurant for my birthday dinner.

residents.d: People who live in a place
residents.e: The residents of my town like to walk around the park.

row.d: A line of things or people next to each other
row.e: The children sat in a row on the bench.

reply.d: To answer or respond to something
reply.e: I will reply to your letter soon.

realized.d: Realized means to understand or become aware of something.
realized.e: I realized I left my backpack at school.

reasonable.d: Reasonable means fair, sensible, and not too much or too little.
reasonable.e: My parents gave me a reasonable bedtime on school nights.

regions.d: Regions are large areas or places, like countries or states.
regions.e: Different regions of the world have different climates.

replaced.d: Replaced means to put something new in the place of something old.
replaced.e: I replaced the old batteries in my toy with new ones.

representatives.d: Representatives are people who speak or act for a group.
representatives.e: The class representatives talked to the teacher about a school dance.

resolution.d: A decision to do something or solve a problem.
resolution.e: The class made a resolution to recycle more at school.

revenue.d: The money that a business or organization earns.
revenue.e: The company's revenue from selling toys was very high last year.

recognized.d: to know or identify something or someone
recognized.e: I recognized my friend's voice on the phone.

recovery.d: the act of getting well again after being sick or hurt
recovery.e: The patient is making a good recovery.

representative.d: someone who speaks or acts for a group
representative.e: The class representative shared our ideas with the teacher.

resistance.d: the act of refusing to accept or do something
resistance.e: The plant showed resistance to the disease.

roman.d: relating to the ancient civilization of Rome
roman.e: The Roman Empire was very powerful.

rome.e: Many tourists visit the city of Rome.

refer.d: To talk about or give information about something.
refer.e: The teacher will refer to the textbook during the lesson.

refused.d: To say no or not agree to do something.
refused.e: My mom refused to let me stay up late.

relief.d: Feeling better after something difficult or painful.
relief.e: I felt relief when the doctor said I was healthy.

replace.d: To put something new in the place of something old.
replace.e: I will replace the batteries in my remote control.

requirements.d: Things that you need to have or do in order to get something.
requirements.e: The school has many requirements for joining the chess club.

respond.d: To answer or react to something.
respond.e: I will respond to my friend's message right away.

random.d: Something that happens without a plan
random.e: The raffle winners were chosen at random.

republican.d: A person who believes in a government run by the people
republican.e: My dad is a republican and votes in elections.

rooms.d: A part of a building with walls and a ceiling
rooms.e: My bedroom is the biggest room in the house.

receiving.d: Getting something that was sent to you.
receiving.e: I'm excited about receiving my new toys in the mail.

regard.d: To think highly of or have respect for.
regard.e: I regard my teacher as very smart and kind.

register.d: To officially record information about yourself.
register.e: I need to register for my new school.

relative.d: A family member, like your mom, dad, brother, or sister.
relative.e: My relative, my aunt, came to visit us last weekend.

reviews.d: What people say about something, like a book or a movie.
reviews.e: The reviews of the new video game were very good.

recognize.d: to know something or someone when you see it
recognize.e: I can recognize my friend's voice on the phone.

recognition.d: When someone notices your work or talents
recognition.e: The teacher gave the student recognition for their hard work.

represents.d: To be an example of something or to stand for it

returns.d: To come back to a place after being away
returns.e: After vacation, the family returns home.

raw.e: The chef used raw vegetables in the salad.

resulting.d: what comes after something else happens

rice.d: a small white or brown grain that is a food
rice.e: My family likes to eat rice with our meals.

riding.d: sitting on and controlling a horse or bike
riding.e: She enjoys riding her bicycle to the park.

rising.e: The sun was rising in the sky.

roads.d: a path for cars and trucks to travel on
roads.e: We drove down the long country roads.

roles.d: the part someone has in a group or activity
roles.e: Each person has an important role on the team.

regularly.d: happening often or at the same time
regularly.e: I brush my teeth regularly, twice a day.

returning.e: After the trip, the family is returning home.

roof.d: the top part of a building that keeps out rain and sun
roof.e: The new roof on the house keeps it dry and warm.

ryan.d: A person's name, a boy's name
ryan.e: Ryan went to the park with his friends.

regulations.e: There are regulations about how to cross the street safely.

repeat.d: To say or do something again
repeat.e: Can you repeat the directions one more time?

researchers.d: People who study and investigate things
researchers.e: The researchers worked hard to learn new information.

recording.d: A way to store sounds or music
recording.e: I made an audio recording of my science presentation.

rent.d: The money you pay to live in a house or apartment
rent.e: My family pays rent every month to live in our home.

racing.d: When people or things go very fast
racing.e: The cars are racing around the track.

rapid.e: The river flows with rapid water.

relation.e: My cousin has a close relation with his grandparents.

rough.e: The old road had a rough surface.

rail.d: A long, narrow piece of wood, metal, or plastic used for support or as a fence.
rail.e: The kids held onto the rail as they climbed the stairs.

reaching.d: Stretching your hand or arm to get something.
reaching.e: The little girl was reaching for the cookie on the top shelf.

rush.d: To move or do something quickly and with energy.
rush.e: The runners rushed across the finish line.
